target_title: Director of Software Engineering, Platform & Infrastructure
summary: Founding engineer turned Director of Engineering at Meridian Logistics, where I grew the team from just myself to 20+ engineers spanning backend, frontend, QA, DevOps, and release management, and delivered the infrastructure behind Meridian Logistics becoming the first regulated fintech platform in the US. I architected the backend systems and processes the platform is built on, took releases from ad hoc to a structured bi-weekly cadence across 4 engineering teams, and led the engineering and compliance work across a 7-year regulatory build-out. Hands-on with Node.js and TypeScript, and equally comfortable shaping technical strategy with C-suite leadership, I'm targeting Director of Engineering and VP of Engineering roles where team development and delivery excellence are priorities.

bullets.text: Built the engineering organization from the ground up, growing from sole backend engineer to a 20+ person team spanning backend, frontend, QA, DevOps, and release management, with full hiring authority and ownership of every career-level and cultural decision.

Architected the microservice backend framework and PostgreSQL schemas Meridian Logistics's platform is built on, containerizing services with Docker and automating deployments via GitHub Actions CI/CD to satisfy regulatory auditability requirements — establishing the foundational engineering standards the team built on as it scaled.

Took on scrum and release management across 4 engineering teams, standardizing sprint and epic planning and replacing ad hoc deployments with a bi-weekly cadence that ran 3× faster than the prior process — delivering predictable, repeatable shipping across 5+ regulated milestones including regulatory the platform approval, custody launch, ETH custody, and clearing and settlement approval.

Partnered with product and cross-functional stakeholders on roadmap, sprint, and release planning across 4 teams, translating business priorities into a consistent bi-weekly delivery cadence across 5+ regulated product milestones.

Piloted AI-assisted development and QA workflows using Claude and Cursor across a 20+ person team in a regulatory-regulated environment, shifting engineers and QA to AI-native tooling and establishing the working patterns the team adopted going forward.

Served as engineering lead for incident response across Meridian Logistics's regulatory-regulated platform, directing technical investigation and cross-functional communication for incidents spanning backend, DevOps, and release teams.

Held a dual engineering and product IC role — defining requirements with stakeholders and contributing to architecture decisions while establishing the QA standards, CI/CD pipelines, and PR annotation practices that governed how the team designed, reviewed, and shipped code.

Introduced a PR annotation practice requiring engineers to comment on their own code in GitHub with explanations of logic and intent, reducing bugs and tech debt by 20% through a final self-review and speeding up peer reviews by giving reviewers richer context, tracked using Jira data on time from in-progress to code-complete.

Built out QA standards and testing protocols with Codecov coverage verification, CI/CD pipelines, and Xray scripts linked to Jira stories, raising test coverage from roughly 50% to 100% as part of peer review gates.

Built internal tooling and software solutions in JavaScript, React.js, HTML, and CSS within an Agile workflow, contributing to automation initiatives that cut engineer onboarding time from over two weeks to three days.
Built Docker automation that reduced engineer onboarding from several weeks to a few days.
Led automation initiatives and built internal tooling to streamline developer onboarding and integration workflows.

Built the frontend media player and user platform in AngularJS, styled with LessCSS and bundled with Grunt, backed by a Node.js/Express API handling cloud storage interactions for user media collections.
Migrated the backend from PHP to a Node.js/MEAN stack.
Managed end-to-end delivery of custom client applications by running weekly stakeholder check-ins, defining requirements, and tracking work velocity in Pivotal Tracker to keep timelines on target.
Built custom client applications — including accounting and reservation software — in PHP and AngularJS across the full delivery cycle.
